Another comet named Padmaketu which is as white as the
fibre of the lotus stalk, rises in the west and is visible for one night.
Its appearance heralds abundance of food as well as happiness for
seven years without interruption.
[Gf. tusk—

The comet named 2varta appearing at midnight in the west
is glossy and ruddy in colour and possessed of a crest turned to the
right or south. It will bring about peace and plenty for mankind
for so many months as the number of MuhQrtas it remains visible.

On Comets XI 139
The comet named Samvarta with its crest smoky and coppery
